skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Create interactive generative art with p5.js, seeded randomness, parameter controls, and reusable HTML templates for browser-based exploration.
skill.summary: Build seeded p5.js generative art with interactive controls and reproducible variations.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high and critical patterns, but review found they are false positives from Apache license text, art terminology, comments, examples, and a JavaScript regex .exec call. The confirmed risks are limited to browser JavaScript templates and external CDN dependencies for p5.js and fonts; no credential access, prompt injection, command execution, or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependencies in Generated Artifacts
low_findings.description: The skill instructs generated HTML artifacts to load p5.js from cdnjs and the template loads Google Fonts. This is expected for browser-based generative art, but it creates a minor supply-chain and network dependency risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are real external browser dependencies. They do not send user data or secrets, so the risk is limited to dependency trust and availability.

low_findings.title: Browser JavaScript Execution Is Core Functionality
low_findings.description: The skill includes JavaScript templates and asks the assistant to generate inline p5.js code. This is legitimate for an interactive art skill, but marketplace users should understand that generated artifacts execute browser JavaScript.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly contain browser-side JavaScript templates. The reviewed code does not include malicious behavior, network exfiltration, or privileged system access.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static detections mapped ordinary words and art concepts such as hash, random, noise, system, pattern, and process to security terms. The cited locations describe art generation, seeded randomness, Apache license language, or template comments, not cryptography or reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines confirms they are natural language, license boilerplate, or harmless comments. No weak cryptographic implementation, system probing, or Windows SAM access is present.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick and exec detections occur in fenced JavaScript examples, Markdown formatting, and a regex .exec call used to parse hex colors. No shell command, child process, Ruby execution, or user-controlled command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only executable-looking call is RegExp.prototype.exec on a color string. The Markdown backticks are documentation syntax and do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: Critical Dangerous-Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined unrelated false positives: CDN links, JavaScript examples, regex .exec, and a comment containing the phrase particle system. Review found no credential access, network exfiltration, obfuscation, or code execution beyond ordinary browser art scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic review specifically checked for credential access and exfiltration indicators and found none. The locations supporting the heuristic are benign in context.

content.value_statement: Generative art projects need reproducible variation, clear controls, and polished presentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create p5.js art with seeded randomness and interactive HTML viewers.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Builds p5.js generative sketches with seeded randomness.
Uses an HTML viewer template with sidebar controls.
Adds parameter sliders, color controls, and seed navigation.
Encourages original artwork instead of copying artists.
limitations: Generated HTML depends on external p5.js and font CDNs unless modified.
The template is a starting point and needs completed art logic.
Visual quality depends on the prompt and implementation review.
It does not provide image recognition or asset editing tools.

use_cases.title: Prototype Generative Visuals
use_cases.description: Turn a visual concept into a p5.js sketch with reproducible seeds and interactive controls.

use_cases.title: Explore Design Systems
use_cases.description: Create controlled visual variations for backgrounds, posters, or motion studies.

use_cases.title: Teach Creative Coding
use_cases.description: Demonstrate flow fields, particles, noise, and parameter tuning through a browser artifact.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Sketch
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one interactive piece.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an original p5.js generative artwork about quiet rainfall. Include a short algorithmic philosophy, seeded randomness, and simple controls.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Parameter Study
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for tunable visual exploration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a generative artwork based on magnetic fields. Add sliders for density, field strength, trail length, and color intensity.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Variation Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a generative system for crystalline growth. Include seed navigation and recommend five seeds with distinct visual character.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Conceptual Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using a detailed creative direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this brief into an interactive p5.js artifact: a city map remembered through ocean currents, soft geometry, and layered time.

output_examples.input: Create an artwork about wind moving through tall grass.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready generative art piece with flowing particles, seed controls, wind strength sliders, and a short philosophy about emergent motion.

output_examples.input: Make a monochrome study of cellular growth.
output_examples.output: An interactive p5.js sketch using seeded growth rules, density controls, reset behavior, and restrained black-and-white rendering.

output_examples.input: Create 100 possible poster backgrounds from one algorithm.
output_examples.output: A seeded generative system with navigation through variations and guidance for selecting strong poster compositions.

best_practices: Review generated HTML before publishing or embedding it.
Use fixed seeds when sharing or reproducing a selected result.
Keep parameter ranges small enough for smooth browser performance.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to copy a living artist or protected artwork.
Do not add unreviewed third-party scripts to generated artifacts.
Do not treat the template as finished without implementing the art logic.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates interactive p5.js generative art with a written concept, seeded randomness, and browser controls.

faq.question: Can it make reproducible outputs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes fixed seeds so the same seed can recreate the same visual result.

faq.question: Does it require a server?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is a single HTML artifact that runs in a browser.

faq.question: Does it use external resources?
faq.answer: Yes. The provided template loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the template complete artwork?
faq.answer: No. The template provides structure and controls, but the generated algorithm must fill in the artwork.
